policy.freeze_decision_rule: Only model_error cases may justify model changes. gold_strictness and state_tradeoff cases do not.

situation_miss.reason: Model likely returns a broad presentation context, while acceptable situation text is narrowly worded.
Situation phrasing is narrower than the likely model summary of the same meeting/presentation context.
Emotion-body case likely yields a broad anxious-morning context rather than the single approved wording.
Situation acceptable list is tighter than the likely model summary of a stressful presentation event.
Case is dominated by thought content; situation wording is underspecified and likely under-accepts valid variants.
Situation is present but approved phrasing is narrow relative to the full text.
Case centers on persistent anxiety and sleep disruption; acceptable situation wording is narrower than likely model output.
Situation likely paraphrased validly by the model, but acceptable wording is constrained.
Emotion-body context is plausible, but gold only allows a small subset of valid situation summaries.
Model likely captures the upcoming presentation context, while gold keeps only one narrow phrasing.
Situation is inferable from the text, but gold is stricter than necessary.
Text clearly describes an upcoming meeting context, but approved situation wording is too narrow.
Emotion-body case likely admits multiple valid situation summaries beyond the current acceptable wording.
High-risk case has broad psychosocial context; approved situation phrases are too specific.

automatic_thought_miss.reason: The text mixes feared outcome and interpretation. Miss likely reflects automatic_thought vs worry_prediction boundary, not a pure extraction failure.
The text contains explicit automatic thought clauses that should be recoverable without relying on broad paraphrase.

behavior_miss.reason: The text mixes body arousal and coping action. Miss likely reflects indirect behavior wording rather than absence of signal.
Sleep disruption is explicitly stated and should be captured as behavior-level evidence.
The text describes impaired concentration, but the acceptable behavior phrasing may be narrower than the model's valid paraphrase.
